headline: Petroleum Revenue Sharing Formula Under First Review
context: Federal government convened special committee in February 2026 to review petroleum revenue sharing formula amid pressure from Sabah and Sarawak.

cards.big: Federal committee reviewing petroleum revenue formula first time since 1974
cards.sub: Sabah and Sarawak demand at least 20% royalty rate from Petronas operations

cards.big: Current 5% royalty generated RM6.2 billion for both states combined in 2025
cards.sub: At 20% the figure would reach approximately RM24.8 billion annually

cards.big: Petronas contributed RM40 billion in dividends to federal government in 2025
cards.sub: Borneo states argue wealth extraction violates MA63 resource ownership terms

cards.big: Terengganu and Kelantan also receive 5% but have not challenged the formula
cards.sub: Constitutional experts say Borneo states have stronger legal basis than Peninsula

cards.big: Revenue sharing tests whether federalism means fair distribution or central extraction
The petroleum revenue formula's first review in decades will test whether federalism adapts or fractures.
